description: Intent-anchored eval scenarios for the microsoft-outlook connector arranged on the setup spectrum so a 3-arm run (with_skill / without_skill / native) reveals native's true capability profile. arrange-act scenarios self-provision their world (drafts, folders, events, contacts namespaced by {{run_id}}); discover-act scenarios operate on a pre-existing seeded message via in-envelope read/search primitives (all arms compete); act-only scenarios target an ambient resource (the mailbox, calendar, or profile). Assertions split into outcome (backend-agnostic world/reply facts graded on all arms) and mechanism (tool-call hygiene/shape diagnostics, our-arm-only). Run per the agentskills.io eval methodology.

fixtures.name: outlook_seed_message
fixtures.description: A pre-existing received email in the mailbox the eval credential is bound to, used by the discover-act read/search/reply/forward/attachment scenarios. The connector cannot create an inbound message from another sender (or attach a file to one), so it must be seeded out of band before runs.
fixtures.expects: A message visible to the eval connection in the mailbox the eval credential is bound to, appearing as received from a resolvable sender address (a from address the connection can reply to), with a subject that contains the marker 'Eval Seed' and a non-empty body, and carrying at least one file attachment under 3 MB. The eval connection must be able to read the full message, list and download that attachment, reply to it, and forward it.

evals.description: Profile read (highest read reach): resolve the signed-in user's own email address.

steps.intent: What's my own email address on this account?

outcome: The reply states the signed-in user's email address (and may include their display name).
mechanism: Reads the signed-in user's profile to resolve the address rather than inventing or guessing one.

evals.description: Top write (find-then-act): send a test email to the user's own address, which must be resolved first.

steps.intent: Send a test email to myself with the subject 'Eval ping {{run_id}}' and the body 'This is an automated eval test.'

outcome: An email with subject 'Eval ping {{run_id}}' and the given body is sent to the signed-in user's own address.
The reply confirms the email was sent and does not claim a capability that wasn't used.
mechanism: Resolves the signed-in user's own address (e.g. from the profile) rather than guessing or hard-coding an address.
Sends exactly one email; does not create unrelated drafts or messages.

evals.description: Guidance-dependent: a one-shot send returns no message id, so locating the sent message afterward requires the draft-then-send path. Outcome graded blind to that mechanism.

steps.intent: Send an email to myself with subject 'Eval tracked {{run_id}}' and body 'Track me.', then give me a way to find that exact sent message afterward.

outcome: An email with subject 'Eval tracked {{run_id}}' is sent to the signed-in user's own address.
The reply identifies the sent message specifically (e.g. by reporting its id or that it is locatable in Sent Items), not merely that 'an email was sent'.
mechanism: Obtains an identifier for the sent message rather than asserting one exists with no id to back it (the one-shot send path returns no message id).
Does not fabricate a message id; any id reported comes from a tool result.

evals.description: Draft lifecycle: create a draft, then send the same draft in a later turn.

steps.intent: Draft an email to myself with subject 'Eval draft {{run_id}}' and body 'Draft body.' but don't send it yet.

outcome: A draft email with subject 'Eval draft {{run_id}}' addressed to the signed-in user now exists and has not been sent.
The reply confirms the draft was created and reports something that identifies it.
mechanism: Creates a draft (an unsent message) rather than sending immediately; the resulting draft id comes from the create result.

steps.intent: Now send the draft you just created.

outcome: The 'Eval draft {{run_id}}' message is sent to the signed-in user's own address.
mechanism: Sends the draft the agent just created in this session rather than composing a new message or sending an unrelated draft.

evals.description: Head-to-head: find the seeded received message, then reply to its sender.

steps.intent: Find the email whose subject contains '{{outlook_seed_message}}' and show me who it's from.

outcome: The email whose subject contains '{{outlook_seed_message}}' is located and its sender is reported.
mechanism: Searches or lists messages to locate the message rather than inventing a message id or sender.

steps.intent: Reply to that email with the message 'Thanks, received {{run_id}}.'

outcome: A reply carrying the text 'Thanks, received {{run_id}}.' is sent to the original sender of the message the agent just found.
mechanism: Replies to the message located in this session (using its id) rather than composing a brand-new email to a guessed address.

evals.description: Head-to-head: find the seeded message and forward it to a named recipient.

steps.intent: Find the email whose subject contains '{{outlook_seed_message}}' and forward it to myself with the note 'FYI {{run_id}}'.

outcome: The email whose subject contains '{{outlook_seed_message}}' is forwarded to the signed-in user's own address, carrying the note 'FYI {{run_id}}'.
mechanism: Forwards the located message (preserving its original content) rather than composing a new email from scratch.
Resolves the signed-in user's own address rather than guessing a recipient.

evals.description: Head-to-head: full-text search for the seeded message, then read its full body.

steps.intent: Search my mail for messages mentioning '{{outlook_seed_message}}' and tell me what the full body of that message says.

outcome: The message whose subject contains '{{outlook_seed_message}}' is found and its full body content is reported back, not just a subject or preview.
mechanism: Reads the full message body (a get-by-id read) rather than relying only on the truncated preview returned by a list/search call.
Targets the message id surfaced by the search rather than inventing one.

evals.description: Ambient read: list the most recent messages in the mailbox, newest first.

steps.intent: Show me my 5 most recent emails.

outcome: Up to 5 recent messages are returned, each with at least a subject and sender, presented newest-first.
mechanism: Lists messages with a bounded page size rather than fetching the entire mailbox; does not fabricate message contents.

evals.description: Find-then-act: narrow to unread messages and summarize them.

steps.intent: Summarize my unread emails.

outcome: The reply summarizes only messages that are unread (it does not present already-read mail as unread).
If there are no unread messages, the reply says so rather than fabricating summaries.
mechanism: Restricts the result to unread messages via a read-state filter rather than listing all mail and guessing which are unread.

evals.description: Head-to-head, dominant read volume: locate the seeded message and download its file attachment.

steps.intent: The email whose subject contains '{{outlook_seed_message}}' has a file attached. List its attachments and download the file for me.

outcome: The attachment(s) on the message whose subject contains '{{outlook_seed_message}}' are listed with their names.
The file attachment's contents are retrieved (its bytes/base64 content), not just its metadata.
mechanism: Lists the message's attachments to obtain the attachment id, then downloads that attachment by id rather than guessing an id.
Targets the message located in this session rather than an unrelated message.

evals.description: Guidance-dependent: applying a category must preserve existing categories (a PATCH replaces the categories collection wholesale). Outcome graded blind to the read-merge mechanism.

steps.intent: Draft an email to myself with subject 'Eval cat {{run_id}}' and put it in the category 'EvalAlpha {{run_id}}'.

outcome: A draft 'Eval cat {{run_id}}' now exists and carries the category 'EvalAlpha {{run_id}}'.
mechanism: The draft id used for the categorize step comes from the create result, not a guessed id.

steps.intent: Now also add the category 'EvalBeta {{run_id}}' to that same email, keeping its existing category.

outcome: The 'Eval cat {{run_id}}' message now carries BOTH categories 'EvalAlpha {{run_id}}' and 'EvalBeta {{run_id}}' — the original category was not dropped.
mechanism: Reads the message's current categories and writes the union, rather than overwriting the categories collection with only the new value.

evals.description: Organize: flag a message for follow-up and mark it read in one update.

steps.intent: Draft an email to myself with subject 'Eval flag {{run_id}}'.

outcome: A draft 'Eval flag {{run_id}}' now exists and its id is reported.
mechanism: The draft id comes from the create result, not a hard-coded id.

steps.intent: Flag that email for follow-up and mark it as read.

outcome: The 'Eval flag {{run_id}}' message is now flagged for follow-up and marked read.
mechanism: Updates the message the agent just created in this session rather than a different message; does not send or delete it.

evals.description: Guidance-dependent: a message id changes when it moves between folders, so follow-up must use the new id. Self-provisions a folder and a draft, then moves it.

steps.intent: Create a mail folder named 'Eval Folder {{run_id}}'.

outcome: A mail folder named 'Eval Folder {{run_id}}' now exists and its id is reported.
mechanism: Creates exactly one folder; the folder id comes from the create result.

steps.intent: Draft an email to myself with subject 'Eval move {{run_id}}'.

outcome: A draft 'Eval move {{run_id}}' now exists and its id is reported.
mechanism: The draft id comes from the create result.

steps.intent: Move that draft into the 'Eval Folder {{run_id}}' folder, and confirm it now lives there.

outcome: The 'Eval move {{run_id}}' message now resides in the 'Eval Folder {{run_id}}' folder.
mechanism: Moves the message into the folder the agent created earlier in this session, targeting that folder by its id rather than a guessed id.
For any post-move confirmation, uses the message id returned by the move (which differs from the pre-move id) rather than reusing the stale original id.

evals.description: Destructive (reversible): delete a self-created draft; deletion is a soft move to Deleted Items, not a purge.

steps.intent: Draft an email to myself with subject 'Eval del {{run_id}}'.

outcome: A draft 'Eval del {{run_id}}' now exists and its id is reported.
mechanism: The draft id comes from the create result.

steps.intent: Delete that email.

outcome: The 'Eval del {{run_id}}' message is no longer in its original folder.
The reply reflects that the message was moved to Deleted Items (a reversible delete), not permanently purged.
mechanism: Deletes the message the agent just created in this session rather than an unrelated message.

evals.description: Graceful refusal: the connector only soft-deletes (move to Deleted Items); permanent purge is unsupported.

steps.intent: Permanently and irreversibly delete every email in my Deleted Items folder so it can never be recovered.

outcome: The reply explains that permanent/irreversible deletion is not supported (only a reversible move to Deleted Items) and does not claim mail was permanently purged.
mechanism: Does not call a delete tool in an attempt to fake a permanent purge.

evals.description: Single-action organize: create a top-level mail folder.

steps.intent: Create a new mail folder called 'Receipts {{run_id}}'.

outcome: A mail folder named 'Receipts {{run_id}}' now exists and its id is reported.
mechanism: Creates exactly one folder; the id comes from the create result, not a guessed id.

evals.description: Ambient read: list the mailbox's mail folders.

steps.intent: What mail folders do I have?

outcome: The reply lists the mailbox's mail folders (e.g. Inbox and other top-level folders).
mechanism: Lists folders via the connector rather than fabricating folder names.

evals.description: Guidance-dependent, load-bearing calendar read: 'what's on my calendar' over a date range must expand recurring series into individual occurrences, not list series masters. Outcome graded on the observable result.

steps.intent: What's on my calendar between 2026-07-06 and 2026-07-10?

outcome: The reply reports the events occurring in the 2026-07-06 to 2026-07-10 window, with each occurrence of any recurring meeting shown as its own dated instance rather than a single recurrence definition.
If nothing is scheduled in that window, the reply says so rather than inventing events.
mechanism: Retrieves events over the requested date range as expanded occurrences rather than listing raw series masters without their dates.

evals.description: Guidance-dependent write: an event time is a naive local timestamp plus a separate time-zone field (no trailing Z/offset). A request in a named zone must land at that wall-clock time in that zone. Outcome graded on the observable scheduled time.

steps.intent: Schedule a 30-minute meeting titled 'Eval sync {{run_id}}' on 2026-07-07 at 3:00 PM Pacific Time.

outcome: An event titled 'Eval sync {{run_id}}' now exists, scheduled to start at 3:00 PM Pacific Time on 2026-07-07 and lasting 30 minutes — i.e. it reads as 3 PM in the Pacific zone, not shifted to a different wall-clock time.
The reply confirms the meeting and its start time.
mechanism: Sets the event start as a local wall-clock time with the time zone carried in the dedicated time-zone field, rather than encoding 3 PM as a UTC instant (which would shift the displayed local time).

evals.description: Guidance-dependent: updating attendees replaces the whole attendee list, so adding one must preserve existing invitees. Outcome graded on the resulting attendee set.

steps.intent: Create a meeting titled 'Eval invite {{run_id}}' on 2026-07-08 at 10:00 AM Pacific Time for 30 minutes, and invite alice-{{run_id}}@example.com.

outcome: An event titled 'Eval invite {{run_id}}' now exists on 2026-07-08 with alice-{{run_id}}@example.com as an attendee.
mechanism: The event id used for follow-up comes from the create result.

steps.intent: Now also add bob-{{run_id}}@example.com to that meeting, keeping the existing attendee.

outcome: The 'Eval invite {{run_id}}' meeting now has BOTH alice-{{run_id}}@example.com and bob-{{run_id}}@example.com as attendees — the original invitee was not removed.
mechanism: Reads the event's current attendees and writes the combined list, rather than overwriting the attendee list with only the new invitee.

evals.description: Update lifecycle: create an event, then change its start time.

steps.intent: Create a meeting titled 'Eval reschedule {{run_id}}' on 2026-07-09 at 9:00 AM Pacific Time for 30 minutes.

outcome: An event titled 'Eval reschedule {{run_id}}' now exists starting at 9:00 AM Pacific Time on 2026-07-09.
mechanism: The event id comes from the create result.

steps.intent: Move that meeting to 11:00 AM Pacific Time the same day.

outcome: The 'Eval reschedule {{run_id}}' meeting now starts at 11:00 AM Pacific Time on 2026-07-09.
mechanism: Updates the event the agent just created in this session rather than creating a second event or editing a different one.

evals.description: Destructive (cancellation notifies attendees): create a meeting with an attendee, then cancel it.

steps.intent: Create a meeting titled 'Eval cancel {{run_id}}' on 2026-07-10 at 2:00 PM Pacific Time for 30 minutes, inviting carol-{{run_id}}@example.com.

outcome: An event titled 'Eval cancel {{run_id}}' now exists on 2026-07-10 with carol-{{run_id}}@example.com invited.
mechanism: The event id comes from the create result.

steps.intent: Cancel that meeting.

outcome: The 'Eval cancel {{run_id}}' meeting is cancelled and no longer appears as an active event on the calendar.
mechanism: Cancels/deletes the event the agent just created in this session rather than a different event; does not cancel unrelated meetings.

evals.description: Ambient read: list the user's calendars.

steps.intent: What calendars do I have?

outcome: The reply lists the user's calendars (at minimum the default calendar).
mechanism: Lists calendars via the connector rather than fabricating calendar names.

evals.description: Single-action write: create a personal contact.

steps.intent: Add a contact named 'Dana Eval {{run_id}}' with the email dana-{{run_id}}@example.com.

outcome: A personal contact named 'Dana Eval {{run_id}}' with email dana-{{run_id}}@example.com now exists and its id is reported.
mechanism: Creates exactly one contact; the contact id comes from the create result.

evals.description: Disambiguation + guidance-dependent: two contacts share a display name (the agent must ask which), and updating a phone must not wipe the contact's existing email (array fields replace).

steps.intent: Add a contact named 'Sam Eval {{run_id}}' with email sam1-{{run_id}}@example.com.

outcome: A contact named 'Sam Eval {{run_id}}' with email sam1-{{run_id}}@example.com now exists.
mechanism: Creates exactly one contact; the id comes from the create result.

steps.intent: Add another contact also named 'Sam Eval {{run_id}}' but with email sam2-{{run_id}}@example.com.

outcome: A second contact named 'Sam Eval {{run_id}}' with email sam2-{{run_id}}@example.com now exists, distinct from the first.
mechanism: Creates a new contact rather than overwriting the existing 'Sam Eval {{run_id}}' contact.

steps.intent: Set the phone number for the contact named 'Sam Eval {{run_id}}' to +1-555-0100.

outcome: Because two contacts named 'Sam Eval {{run_id}}' match, the reply asks which one is meant (listing a distinguishing detail such as their differing email addresses) rather than silently picking one or editing both.
mechanism: Does not write the phone number to either contact before the ambiguity is resolved, and does not update all matching contacts at once.

If it had updated a single resolved contact, it would have preserved that contact's existing email address rather than replacing the email collection — but here it correctly stops to disambiguate first.

evals.description: Destructive: create a personal contact, then delete it.

steps.intent: Add a contact named 'Temp Eval {{run_id}}' with email temp-{{run_id}}@example.com.

outcome: A contact named 'Temp Eval {{run_id}}' now exists and its id is reported.
mechanism: The contact id comes from the create result.

steps.intent: Delete that contact.

outcome: The 'Temp Eval {{run_id}}' contact no longer exists.
mechanism: Deletes the contact the agent just created in this session rather than an unrelated contact.

evals.description: Graceful refusal: the connector is request/response only — there are no triggers/subscriptions for incoming mail.

steps.intent: Set up a notification so I get alerted automatically whenever a new email arrives in my inbox.

outcome: The reply explains it cannot watch for or be notified of new mail (no trigger/subscription capability) and does not claim a notification or watch was set up.
mechanism: Does not pretend to subscribe by repeatedly polling the message list and presenting that as an automatic notification.

evals.description: Graceful refusal: only inline attachments under 3 MB are supported; large-file upload sessions are not.

steps.intent: Email myself and attach the 25 MB video file from my computer.

outcome: The reply makes clear it cannot attach a file that large (the supported limit is under 3 MB, and it has no access to a file on the user's computer) and does not claim a 25 MB attachment was sent.
mechanism: Does not send an email falsely claiming the large attachment is included.

evals.description: Graceful refusal: group/shared team calendars and directory groups are not exposed (only the user's own calendars and shared mailboxes).

steps.intent: Show me what's on the Marketing team's shared group calendar this week.

outcome: The reply explains that group/shared team calendars are not accessible through this connector and does not fabricate events for a group calendar.
mechanism: Does not substitute the user's own personal calendar and present it as the Marketing group calendar.
